title: A topographic atlas defines developmental origins of cell heterogeneity in the human embryonic lung
description: Figure 1 from Sountoulidis et al., Nature Cell Biology 2023. Multi-panel figure combining UMAP clustering of 163,000 cells into 83 states, spatial transcriptomics mapping, cell-cell co-occurrence network, and WNT7B signaling validation.
source: Sountoulidis et al., Nature Cell Biology, 2023

L2_specific: UMAP clustering shows 83 distinct cell types in human embryonic lung
spatial distribution of cell niches across lung tissue
network graph reveals cell-cell co-occurrence interactions
WNT7B signaling drives airway smooth muscle progenitor proliferation
L3_detailed: topographic atlas maps developmental trajectories of cell populations in human embryonic lung using single-cell RNA sequencing combined with spatial transcriptomics

multi-panel figure integrates UMAP, spatial maps, co-occurrence networks, and fluorescence validation to characterize 83 cell states across mesenchyme, endothelium, epithelium, and immune compartments
